action.reasoning: Observation: 10 iterations of segfault (exit 139) across various models, batch sizes, and data subsets. Even SimpleMLP on CPU with 2000 graphs crashes. Only test_env with 128 graphs succeeds, suggesting a latent problem with PyG DataLoader or GPU memory that prevents reproducible training at scale. Analysis: The consistent segfaults indicate the current stack (PyG + GPU) is unreliable for this task. A safer baseline uses precomputed graph-level features (mean, std, histograms of node/edge attributes) and a standard MLP classifier, avoiding PyG entirely for training. This guarantees execution and yields a first metric. Decision: Write a preprocess script to extract fixed-size graph features from the full dataset (CPU, chunked), save to .npz, then train an MLP on these features. Update predict.py to compute features on-the-fly for test data. Submit eval after 10-epoch training to finally obtain a baseline AP and measure per-epoch time.
action.command: mkdir -p processed && python preprocess_features.py && python train.py --epochs 10
